description: Instance D (Transcription, deductive lower bound): formally verified Rocq proofs of textbook arithmetic (e.g. commutativity of + on nat). Dominant coherent-and-wrong: the prover discharges (Qed.) an adjacent weaker claim (e.g. right-identity n+0=n instead of n+m=m+n) under the intended theorem name, or smuggles an axiom.

sources.name: Axiom auditor (rocqchk --print-assumptions)
sources.notes: must report 'Closed under the global context'; rejects Admitted/Axiom

sources.name: Prior-proofs registry + signature (invariant plane)
sources.notes: proof_registry/*.v + registry.sig; recompiled each cycle (no regression)

sources.name: Exerciser's false-theorem mutations
sources.notes: mutation_matrix.json + mutation_*.v; each must FAIL to compile

actors.name: Gate sentinel / orchestrator
actors.builds: plan approval, gate verdicts, the axiom audit, and the loop's SL-1.0 instance-d.sl.json

upper_bound.text: the manifest clauses it gates the plan and coverage against

lower_bound.text: registry recompiled (no regression), proof compiled, axiom audit clean, every mutation rejected

forbidden_moves: alter the manifest, stdlib, or registry after dispatch
skip the axiom audit or the mutation catchability check

actors.name: Theorem formalizer
actors.builds: the formal theorem statement (the precise upper bound) from the English exercise

upper_bound.text: the textbook exercise and its scope clauses

lower_bound.text: expressibility: the statement must type-check under coqc with the stdlib

forbidden_moves: append a proof / Qed to the statement
read the prover's proof or the mutations

actors.name: Tactical prover
actors.builds: the tactical proof that discharges the formalizer's statement

upper_bound.text: the immutable formal statement it must discharge unchanged

lower_bound.text: compiles to Qed under coqc + stdlib and passes the axiom audit (no Admitted/Axiom)

actors.name: Mutation exerciser
actors.builds: false-theorem mutations that the kernel must reject

upper_bound.text: the manifest clauses and the formal statement it perturbs into falsehoods

lower_bound.text: each mutation must FAIL to compile under coqc + stdlib (it is genuinely false)

disjointness.rationale: The formalizer holds only the English manifest (never the proof or mutations); the prover holds only the formal statement and the kernel (never the mutations); the exerciser holds the manifest and the statement and authors falsehoods the kernel must reject (never the proof). The deductive kernel (coqc + rocqchk) is the immutable lower bound none of them can alter, and the registry pins prior proofs against regression.
disjointness.load_bearing_barrier: the prover never sees the mutations and the exerciser never sees the proof; the formalizer sees neither (Unix 0700 homedirs; the prover imports the statement one-way via coqc Require only)

catchability.blind_spot: a vacuous or inconsistent statement (e.g. a false hypothesis that trivialises the goal)
catchability.via: a vacuous-hypothesis mutation that should be rejected; the kernel/axiom audit catches smuggled axioms

catchability.blind_spot: discharges an adjacent weaker theorem under the intended name
catchability.via: identity-flip mutations the kernel must reject; type cross-check of the proof against the statement

catchability.blind_spot: omits a mutation for some scope clause
catchability.via: Gate C requires every manifest clause to appear in some mutation's target_clauses
